Measuring AI performance in terms of the length of HCAST: Human-Calibrated Autonomy Software task and RE (Research Engineering) task (measured by how long they take by human professionals) that generalist frontier model agents can complete autonomously with 50% reliability. It has been doubling approximately every 7 months for the last 6 years. Extrapolating this trend predicts that, in under a decade, we will see AI agents that can independently complete a large fraction of software tasks that currently take humans days or weeks.

Measuring AI performance in terms of the length of SWE-Bench Verified Tasks (measured by how long they take by human professionals) that generalist frontier model agents can complete autonomously with 50% reliability. It has been doubling approximately every 7 months for the last 6 years. Extrapolating this trend predicts that, in under a decade, we will see AI agents that can independently complete a large fraction of software tasks that currently take humans days or weeks.

Measuring AI performance in terms of the length of tasks (measured by how long they take by human professionals) that generalist frontier model agents can complete autonomously with 50% reliability. It has been doubling approximately every 7 months for the last 6 years. Extrapolating this trend predicts that, in under a decade, we will see AI agents that can independently complete a large fraction of software tasks that currently take humans days or weeks.

Measuring AI performance in terms of the length of tasks (measured by how long they take by human professionals) that generalist frontier model agents can complete autonomously with 80% reliability. It has been doubling approximately every 7 months for the last 6 years. Extrapolating this trend predicts that, in under a decade, we will see AI agents that can independently complete a large fraction of software tasks that currently take humans days or weeks.

Average token cost for AGI models worldwide.Costs have plummeted ~120x overall, from $60/M tokens in 2021 to ~$0.5/M in 2025, with median annual reductions of 50x post-2024 due to commoditization (e.g., open-source Llama models) and efficiency gains (e.g., quantization). The speed of becoming cost-effective accelerated in 2024–2025, with 10–200x yearly drops tied to inference optimizations reducing compute needs by 4–30x

Represents throughput of AI model inference, measured as tokens per second (TPS), the number of output tokens a model can generate every second. It’s a key indicator of model speed, responsiveness, and scalability for both user-facing and agentic (multi-step reasoning or tool-using) tasks.Speeds have increased exponentially, from ~15 TPS in 2021 (e.g., GPT-3 on A100 GPUs) to over 1,000 TPS in 2025 (e.g., Llama 4 Maverick on Blackwell/Cerebras). This ~67x improvement is driven by hardware (e.g., Blackwell FP4 support), optimizations (e.g., speculative decoding, KV caching), and architectural advances (e.g., MoE in Llama 4). Early models were serial and memory-bound; modern ones achieve real-time (human-reading-speed ~10–20 TPS) or superhuman rates for agentic tasks.

Amortized hardware cost plus energy cost for the final training run of frontier models. The selected models are among the top 10 most compute-intensive for their time. Amortized hardware costs are the product of training chip-hours and a depreciated hardware cost, with 23% overhead added for cluster-level networking.

ARC-AGI benchmark presents AI systems with a set of independent tasks. Each task includes demonstration or input pairs followed by one or more test or output scenarios . This benchmark emphasizes generalized learning ability: It is impossible for systems to prepare in advance, as each task introduces a unique logic. The tasks require no specialized world knowledge or language skills but instead draw on assumed prior knowledge, such as the concept of objects, basic topology, and elementary arithmetic—concepts typically mastered by children at an early age.

Estimated cloud compute costs for the final training run of frontier models. The selected models are among the top 10 most compute-intensive for their time. The costs are the product of the number of training chip-hours and a historical cloud rental price.
Leading ML hardware becomes 40% more energy-efficient each year.The upcoming Blackwell series of processors may be even more efficient, depending on their power consumption.

GSM8K, introduced by OpenAI in 2021, is a dataset containing approximately 8,000 diverse grade-school math word problems that challenges AI models to generate multistep solutions using arithmetic operations.

HumanEval, a benchmark introduced by OpenAI researchers in 2021, evaluates the coding abilities of AI systems through 164 challenging, handwritten programming problem.The current leader in HumanEval performance is Claude 3.5 Sonnet (HPT), which achieved a score of 100%

Inference costs refer to the expense of querying a trained model, and they are typically measured in USD per million tokens. Data on AI token pricing comes from both Artificial Analysis and Epoch AI’s proprietary database on API pricing. The reported price is a 3:1 weighted average of input and output token price

Inference costs refer to the expense of querying a trained model, and they are typically measured in USD per million tokens. Data on AI token pricing comes from both Artificial Analysis and Epoch AI’s proprietary database on API pricing. The reported price is a 3:1 weighted average of input and output token price
MATH is a dataset of 12,500 challenging, competitionlevel mathematics problems introduced by UC Berkeley and University of Chicago researchers in 2021. Human Baseline is 90%.

Hardware computational performance shown in floating-point operations per second (FLOP/s) per US dollar, adjusted for inflation.FLOP/s values refer to 32-bit (full) precision. Data is expressed in constant 2024 US$. Inflation adjustment is based on the US
Consumer Price Index (CPI).This measures computing power per dollar—specifically, how many calculations per second you get for each inflation-adjusted dollar when buying a GPU.

The Massive Multitask Language Understanding (MMLU) benchmark assesses model performance in zero-shot or fewshot scenarios across 57 subjects, including the humanities,STEM, and the social sciences (Figure 2.2.3). MMLU has emerged as a premier benchmark for assessing LLM capabilities: Many state-of-the-art models like GPT-4o, Claude 3.5, and Gemini 2.0 have been evaluated against MMLU.

Introduced in 2019 by researchers from the University of Washington and the Allen Institute for AI, the Visual Commonsense Reasoning (VCR) challenge tests the commonsense visual reasoning abilities of AI systems. In this challenge, AI systems not only answer questions based on images but also reason about the logic behind their answers (Figure 2.3.1). Performance in VCR is measured using the Q->AR score, which evaluates the machine’s ability to both select the correct answer to a question (Q->A) and choose the appropriate rationale behind that answer (Q->R).

Training compute of frontier AI models. Since 2010, the training compute used to create AI models has been growing at a rate of 4.4x per year. Most of this growth comes from increased spending, although improvements in hardware have also played a role.

Electricity/Power consumption for training major AI models.Training frontier models requires a large and growing amount of power for GPUs, servers, cooling and other equipment. This is driven by an increase in GPU count; power draw per GPU is also growing

Across all domains of AI and ML, models are using more and more training data. In language modeling, datasets are growing at a rate of 3.7x per year. The largest models currently use datasets with tens of trillions of words.

Annual Installations/Sales of Industrialrobots in Food Industry
Annual Installations/Sales of Industrialrobots in Automotive Sector

Asia is the world’s largest industrial robot market. In 2024, 401,665 units were installed,up 5% year over year. 74% of all newly deployed robots were installed in Asia (2023:70%). From 2019 to 2024, annual robot installations grew by 10% on average each year.Three of the top five markets for industrial robots are in Asia: China is by far the largest market. More than every other robot (54%) installed worldwide in 2024 ended up in China: Installations were up 7% to 295,045 units.

Agriculture category includes robots used for tasks such as plowing, seeding, harvesting, weeding, fertilizing, and pesticide spraying—both indoors (e.g. greenhouses) and outdoors (e.g. fields and vineyards). It also covers robots for milking, as well as those used in other livestock activities like feeding and barn cleaning.
Annual Installations/Sales of Professional Service robots

Hospitality category includes robots that prepare and serve food or drinks, as well as those that provide information, guidance, or remote presence in customer-facing environments like hotels, restaurants, and museums.

Medical and health care category includes robots used in clinical and care settings for diagnostics, surgery, rehabilitation, and non-invasive therapy. It also includes hospital support robots, wearable exoskeletons, and telepresence robots used specifically in healthcare.

Professional cleaning category includes robots designed to clean floors, windows, walls, tanks, pipes, and vehicle hulls in professional environments. This category also includes disinfection robots and others used for specialized or large-scale cleaning tasks.

Transportation and logistics category includes robots that transport goods and manage inventory in both indoor and outdoor environments. These robots are used in places such as warehouses, hospitals, hotels, and public streets, and they support activities like deliveries, stock counting, and restocking.

description: Wholesale cost per 12V lead-acid SLI battery (60Ah, 0.72kWh). Mature technology with stable costs. China factory prices ~$25, USA/Europe ~$55-65 due to higher costs. Minimal cost reduction expected.
source: Made-in-China.com wholesale prices (MOQ 100-300), Jan 2026. China: 60Ah $21-33, 75Ah $26-37. USA/Europe prices estimated at 2-2.5x China due to labor, logistics, environmental compliance.

description: Wholesale cost per 12V LiFePO4 SLI battery (60Ah, 0.77kWh). Includes integrated BMS. Currently 4-6x lead-acid cost but declining 15-18%/year. Crossover expected 2028-2032 by region.
source: CBB Battery, Made-in-China.com wholesale (MOQ 100), Jan 2026. China: 60Ah LFP $150-220. Projected 15-18% annual decline following pack-level learning rates. BMS costs declining with scale.
